# Smoke Testing

## Quick Run (fully automated)

```powershell
py -m tests.smoke_helpers [--raw-dir raw] [--output-dir output] [--folders 260330,260407] [--trigger "user request"]
```

Runs all 6 phases, writes a timestamped report to `smoke-reports/`.

## Pipeline Phases

| # | Phase | What it does |
|---|-------|-------------|
| 0 | Python tests | `pytest --tb=short -q` (300s timeout) |
| 1 | ETL runs | First folder = full load, rest = incremental syncs via `cli.run()` |
| 2 | Output verification | 13 Parquet files, ETL run history, entity counts, wine schema, dossiers, validation |
| 3 | Data integrity | 13 FK constraints + 6 dossier linkage checks (bidirectional wine↔dossier, tracked↔companion) |
| 4 | Cross-run consistency | Wine count trend, change log growth, ETL run count |
| 5 | MCP integration | Rebuild server, test tools (including pairing_candidates) + resources over stdio |

## Module Reference

All code lives in `tests/smoke_helpers/`. See [detailed API](./references/api.md) for function signatures and dataclass fields.

| Module | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `__init__.py` | Shared dataclasses: `SmokeConfig`, `RunResult`, `CheckResult` |
| `discover.py` | Auto-discover YYMMDD folders, validate CSVs, get environment info |
| `runner.py` | Execute ETL (`cli.run()` in-process), run pytest, rebuild MCP server |
| `verify.py` | DuckDB-based output checks, FK integrity, dossier linkage |
| `mcp_checks.py` | Async MCP client — tool & resource smoke tests over stdio |
| `report.py` | Generate & write timestamped Markdown reports |
| `__main__.py` | CLI orchestrator — wires all modules together |

## Raw Folder Convention

Each `raw/<YYMMDD>/` folder must contain 3 CSVs: `export-wines.csv`, `export-bottles-stored.csv`, `export-bottles-gone.csv`. Folders are auto-discovered and sorted chronologically; earliest = full load, rest = syncs.

## Key Patterns

- All verification functions return `CheckResult(name, passed, details, data)`
- ETL runs return `RunResult` with structured metrics (no stdout parsing needed)
- MCP tests spawn the server as a subprocess and connect via `mcp.client.ClientSession`
- Reports are generated by `report.py` using the same dataclasses — no manual formatting needed
- `price_observation.parquet` is optional (only exists if price tracking data has been recorded)

## Expected Behaviours (not failures)

- Duplicate natural key warnings on stderr — expected for wines with multiple Vinocell entries
- `reload_data` MCP tool reports "CSV file not found" — CSVs aren't in the expected location during smoke tests
- Dynamic MCP tools (companion dossiers, price tracking) are skipped when no tracked wines exist in the dataset
- Exit code 1 is acceptable when the only issues are warnings
